translations.descricao: Schizophrenia is a heterogeneous syndrome that affects multiple dimensions of the patient's life, without a cure, but controllable with pharmacological and psychosocial therapies. Inconsistent definitions of treatment resistance limit research and clinical practice. Approximately 30% of patients do not respond to two adequate trials of antipsychotics, characterizing treatment-resistant schizophrenia (TRS). Consensuses emphasize the need for a multidimensional approach, including monitoring of physical health due to high comorbidities.
translations.sus.indicacao: Comprehensive treatment in SUS for persistent psychotic symptoms, including antipsychotics and psychosocial rehabilitation in Psychosocial Care Centers (CAPS)
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults with a diagnosis of schizophrenia or psychotic disorders, prioritizing resistant cases
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: Diagnosis based on consensual criteria for response and resistance; initial treatment with antipsychotics, clozapine for TRS after failure of two agents
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: Global lifetime prevalence of 0.3-0.7% in adults
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Annual incidence of 10-22 cases per 100,000 adults
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Excess mortality with standardized mortality ratio 2-3 times higher due to physical comorbidities and suicide
